titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Burton Adams Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid created in accordance with manuscripts and archives' processing manual.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 1, 2018 at 07:00

did.abstract: Professor of history at Yale University. Correspondence concerning English history, the Yale History Dept., the relationship of graduate to undergraduate education at Yale and in general, the American Historical Association, and the American Historical Review. Includes correspondence with prominent British and American historians.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Burton Adams Papers (MS 30). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of George B. Adams and Ruth Adams, in 1942.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The George Burton Adams Collection consists of approcimately 6,000 items.

The Papers of George Burton Adams are filed according to the subjects described on the following pages. The original arrangement of Professor Adams has been retained. Because only a part of Adams' papers were given to the library, many of the numerical files are nonexistent. The original numbering has been preserved.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Professor of history at Yale University, 1888-1925; Ph.D. University of Leipzig, 1886; editor of American History Review, 1895-1913; editor and author of numerous books and articles.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The original arrangement of Professor Adams has been retained. Because only a part of Adams' papers were given to the library, many of the numerical files are nonexistent. The original numbering has been preserved for files 1-294. File numbering for files 295-315 was supplied by staff during processing.

did.unittitle: American Historical Association Secondary School Committee
French history - "Story of the French Nation"
Transfer of French Nation ([Flood and Vincent] Macmillan)
American Historical Association, American Historical Review and the question of their union.
Watson's French history; introduction to Green - Short History of England
Jurisprudence section of American Historical Association
Union of American Historical Association and American Historical Review
Requests to give addresses in various places
Revision of text for high schools, especially French history
Election of managing editor of American Historical Review, et cetera
Historical fiction, Bemont of Monod and other publications
American Historical Association; membership, cost of magazine, and meeting in New Orleans 1903
Combination of American Historical Review with Carnegie Institute
Yale University Library budget and Library Committee
John A. Porter Prise at Yale
Unfiled letters from others - "Comments on the "Present Problems of Medieval History""
Unfiled letters from others - "Comments on the "Present Problems of Medieval History""
Carnegie Institute; American Historical Institute, London
Edward G. Bourne; John S. Bassett
Charles Seymour, Robert Moses, Stewart L. Mims.
Proposed American Quarterly of European History
Comments on G. B. A. works
George Louis Beer; British Empire and Peace negotiations
Students; colleagues; "Constitutional History of England' (4 folders)
Notes for book on British Empire
G. B. A. notes and drafts
Review of Forrest's "Development of Western Civilasation"
G. B. A. Presidential Address (American Historical Association)
Day, George Peraley, Dean Oertal; role of graduate schools
Edwin S. Corwin, "An Examination of the Covenant"
New York University Hall of Fame

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Asakawa, Yale President Arthur Twining Hadley, and others regarding Asakawa's research leave and continued appointment in the Yale History Dept. during a time of "appalling deficit" confronting the university.

did.unittitle: Benjamin Terry; "Origins of the English Constitution"
Comments on G. B. A. articles.
Notes and comments on G. B. A. articles
Henry Holt and Company; Felik Alcan
"European History; An Outline of its Development", notices and reviews
Venezuela question - Monroe Doctrine and Feeling toward England
"British Empire and League of Peace" comments
"Outline Sketch of English Constitutional History" comments
"The British Empire and A League of Peace" comments
"The British Empire and A League of Peace" comments
"Federal Government: Its Method and Function"; also Free Traders.
Irish question; League of Nations; clippings
Last chapter of "Preservation of American Colleges"
Bill to G. B. A. from Public Records Office
Photostats of documents from Public Records Office, London
Miscellaneous notes of G. B. A.
